info.title: Rita API
info.description:
Rita API Server for the RITA Go application.

## Authentication
Most endpoints require authentication via Keycloak JWT token or session cookie.

## Organization Context
All data is scoped to the user's active organization (tenant).

## Real-time Updates
For real-time updates, connect to the SSE endpoint at `/api/sse/events`.
See AsyncAPI spec for event documentation.

message.example: Signup successful. Please check your email for verification instructions.

message.description: Generic response (does not reveal if email exists)
message.example: If a pending verification exists, a new email has been sent

email.description: Email address to resend verification to

accessToken.description: Keycloak access token from client-side authentication

deletedSessions.description: Number of sessions that were destroyed

message.example: Email verified successfully. You can now sign in.

token.description: Email verification token from the verification link

next_cursor.description: Cursor for next page, null if no more pages

message.example: How do I reset my password?

content.example: How do I reset my password?

latest_options.description: Options returned from verification (e.g., available spaces)

active.description: Whether this model is currently active

MlModel.description: Active ML model or null if none active

post.description: Creates a pending user and triggers webhook for email verification. User must verify email before they can log in.

400.description: Validation error or email already exists

post.description: Resends the verification email for pending signups. Rate limited to 1 request per 5 minutes per email.

200.description: Request processed (does not reveal if email exists)

post.description: Creates a session cookie from a Keycloak access token. Used for browser-based flows where cookies are needed (e.g., SSE).

delete.description: Destroys the current session and clears the session cookie.
Destroys all sessions for the current user across all devices. Requires a valid session.

post.description: Verifies a signup token from the email verification link. Marks user as ready for Keycloak signin.

400.description: Invalid/expired token or account already exists

get.description: Returns the current session status and user info if authenticated.
Returns the current authenticated user's profile information.

patch.description: Updates the authenticated user's firstName and/or lastName.

400.description: Validation error or no fields to update

get.description: List all ticket clusters for the organization with ticket counts

schema.description: Time period filter for ticket counts

parameters.description: Time period filter for ticket counts
parameters.name: period

get.description: Get cluster metadata including KB articles count and ticket counts
Get paginated list of tickets in a cluster with filtering and sorting
Get knowledge base articles linked to a cluster
List user's chat conversations with pagination
Get paginated messages for a conversation using cursor-based pagination

post.description: Send a new message to a conversation. Triggers webhook for processing.

delete.description: Delete a conversation and all its messages

get.description: List all data source connections for the organization

post.description: Create a new data source connection
Create default data source configurations for the organization (idempotent)

get.description: Get a single data source connection by ID

put.description: Update an existing data source connection

post.description: Initiate credential verification for a data source. Returns immediately with status "verifying". Final result arrives via SSE event `data_source_update`.
Initiate document sync from the data source. Returns immediately with status "syncing". Progress and completion arrive via SSE event `data_source_update`.

Initiate ITSM ticket ingestion for Autopilot clustering. Only valid for ITSM data sources (servicenow_itsm, jira_itsm). Returns HTTP 202 Accepted. Progress arrives via SSE event `ingestion_run_update`.

get.description: Returns the currently active ML model for the user's organization

200.description: Active model or null if none active

get.description: Retrieve a single ticket by its ID
Check API server health and service status
